description: The 2018 debate regarding the amendment of Section 25 of the South African Constitution to allow for land expropriation without compensation. The proposal was highly divisive, reflecting deep historic inequality.

key_events.description: Parliamentary committee releases findings showing broad public support for amendment.
International investor concern causes Rand volatility, shifting moderate opinion.
ANC leadership faces pressure from coalition partners to formalize the policy.
Final legislative hurdles prove too high to secure necessary supermajority.

calibration_notes: Demonstrates the volatility of economic-political debates where international pressure influences domestic sentiment.
